description: Anchor lens for the Greek concept of synesis (σύνεσις) — integrative comprehension, the act of bringing disparate knowledge into a coherent understanding. Distinct from episteme (systematic demonstration), epignosis (relational knowing), and sophia (practical wisdom). No single English equivalent: 'insight' and 'understanding' both capture partial aspects only. Grounds in logos.synesis.insight via grc-core-cog-022 (ADR-0073c; no EN-collapse edge — absence is intentional, documented by same ADR). Cognitive mode: integrative-comprehension. Closes the Greek epistemic quad: sophia / epignosis / episteme / synesis.
